resources.string: Choose from the options below and enjoy an ad–free experience
The base station address information comes from the Internet and is for reference only!
To obtain address information, you need to enable positioning permissions
RSSI in ASU， Asu is calculated based on 3GPP RSSI.
RSRP in ASU，Asu is calculated based on 3GPP RSRP.
RSCP in ASU， Asu is calculated based on 3GPP RSRP.
Sorry, failed to retrieve application information
Positioning permission needs to be enabled to obtain Bluetooth information
To obtain Bluetooth information, you need to use the Nearby Devices permission
Please allow Bluetooth scanning permission to be enabled in order to discover nearby Bluetooth devices.
Your device does not support Bluetooth.
This signal is not real-time and changes depending on the refresh rate of the Bluetooth list.
To scan for nearby Bluetooth devices, you need to turn on location services first.
Query whether or not the device supports multiple Access point (AP) which are bridged together
Loading error. Touch here to retry.

•Definition: \\nA frequency band refers to a specific continuous frequency range that is allocated for a specific communication technology or application. In cellular networks, each frequency band has a unique number (such as Band 1 for LTE and n78 for 5G) and is assigned by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) or regional organizations (such as 3GPP). \\n\\n•Function: \\nDifferent frequency bands have different propagation characteristics (such as penetration ability and coverage range) and are suitable for different scenarios. \\nFor example: \\n\\nLow frequency band (such as 700MHz): wide coverage, strong wall penetration ability, suitable for wide area coverage. Mid-frequency band (such as 1.8GHz-2.6GHz): balances coverage and capacity, and is the main frequency band for 4G/5G. High frequency band (such as above 3.5GHz): large bandwidth and high capacity, but small coverage, suitable for hot spots. Operators optimize network performance by deploying multiple frequency bands.
The connection status of this cell
Approximate time this cell information was received from the modem.
To obtain base station location data, you need to use the positioning function. Please enable positioning permission.
You need to enable positioning permission before you can download base station address data.

•Definition:\\nThe center frequency is the middle value of the frequency range of the frequency band, and the calculation formula is:\\nCenter frequency = (lowest frequency + highest frequency) / 2\\n\\n•Function:\\nIn wireless communication, signals are usually modulated and demodulated based on the center frequency. \\nThe center frequency is used to calculate channel spacing and frequency deviation (for example, in 5G NR, ARFCN is directly related to the center frequency).
Are you sure you want to change the software language to %s? \n It may take some time to download the language pack.
Choose the application to open the file
%1$s won\'t work unless you enable Google Play services.
%1$s won\'t run without Google Play services, which are missing from your device.
%1$s is having trouble with Google Play services. Please try again.
%1$s won\'t run without Google Play services, which are not supported by your device.
%1$s won\'t run unless you update Google Play services.
%1$s won\'t run without Google Play services, which are currently updating.
New version of Google Play services needed. It will update itself shortly.
Corrected text input cannot be empty
Channel Quality Index (CQI). Value is between 0 and 15 (inclusive)
Get the table index of the channel quality indicator. Value is between 1 and 6 (inclusive).

List of CSI channel quality indicators (CQI) for all subbands. If the CQI report is for the entire wideband, a single CQI index is provided. If the CQI report is for all subbands, one CQI index is provided for each subband, in ascending order of subband index.
CSI channel quality indicator (CQI) table index. There are multiple CQI tables.Value is between 1 and 3 inclusive.
CSI reference signal received power. Range: –156 dBm to –31 dBm.
CSI reference signal received quality.Range: –20 dB to –3 dB.
CSI Signal to Noise and Interference Ratio, Range: –23 dB to 23 dB.
Signal strength as dBm.min(CDMA RSSI, EVDO RSSI) of the measured cell.
Signal strength as dBm.the RSSI of the measured cell.
Get the dBm value of SS–RSRP, range: –140..–44dBm
RSVP as dBm value –120..–24 dBm or unavailable.
2. How to protect your network from WLAN squatting
(1) Set MAC addresses allowed on your router
(2) Disable SSID broadcast for your router
Get the emergency number list based on current locale, sim, default, modem and network.

Returns the number of logical modems currently configured to be activated. Returns 0 if none of voice, sms, data is not supported Returns 1 for Single standby mode (Single SIM functionality). Returns 2 for Dual standby mode (Dual SIM functionality). Returns 3 for Tri standby mode (Tri SIM functionality).
The device does not support humidity sensor, please enter the humidity value manually.
The device does not support air pressure sensor, please enter the air pressure value manually.
The device does not support temperature sensors, please enter the temperature value manually.
The device does not support temperature, humidity sensors, and air pressure sensors. Please enter parameter values manually to complete the humidity calculation.
The device does not support this sensor

•Definition:\\nDownlink frequency refers to the frequency used when the base station sends data to the mobile phone (i.e. \"downlink\"). In FDD (frequency division duplex) mode, downlink and uplink use different frequencies; in TDD (time division duplex) mode, uplink and downlink use the same frequency but different time slots. \\n•Characteristics:\\nUsually higher than uplink frequency (e.g. LTE Band 1\'s downlink frequency is 2110–2170MHz, and the uplink frequency is 1920–1980MHz). \\nThe strength of the downlink signal received by the mobile phone directly affects the download speed and signal quality.
Whether the device supports Dual Band Simultaneous (DBS) operation
Wi–Fi Easy Connect DPP AKM enables provisioning and configuration of Wi–Fi devices without the need of using the device PSK passphrase.
true if this device supports Wi–Fi Easy Connect (DPP) Enrollee Responder mode.
Ec/No (energy per chip divided by noise spectral density) in dB. The Ec/No range of the measurement cell is [–24, 1].
The function of adding pictures is only available for email sending.
true if this device supports Wi–Fi Enhanced Open (OWE)
Wrong text input cannot be empty
Your encouragement is the driving force for us to move forward infinitely~
Signal to noise ratio. Valid values are 0–8. 8 is the highest.
Found %d devices connected to this Wi–Fi

•Definition:\\nFrequency range refers to the interval between the lowest frequency and the highest frequency of the frequency band, in MHz or GHz. For example:\\nThe frequency range of LTE Band 3 is 1710–1880MHz (uplink: 1710–1785MHz, downlink: 1805–1880MHz). \\nThe frequency range of 5G NR n78 is 3300–3800MHz (TDD mode, uplink and downlink sharing). \\n•Function:\\nThe wider the frequency range, the more data can be carried (bandwidth determines theoretical capacity). \\nDifferent countries/regions may allocate different frequency ranges to the same frequency band (e.g. LTE Band 7 uses 2600–2690MHz in some regions).
Weak signal. Move to an open area outside for better reception.
Returns the Group Identifier Level 1 for a GSM phone.
Has the calling application been granted carrier privileges by the carrier.
Used to check if an ICC card is present in the device. An ICC card is a smart card containing a Subscriber Identity Module (SIM) that is used to identify and authenticate mobile network subscribers.
Whether the device is currently on a technology (e.g. UMTS or LTE) which can support voice and data simultaneously. This can change based on location or network condition.
Data capable means that this device supports packet–switched data connections over the telephony network.
Whether to use mobile data according to user settings
Whether the device currently supports RTT (Real Time Text). Depends on the carrier\'s support for this feature and the device\'s firmware support.
Whether the device is a world phone.

Base station latitude, which is a decimal number as specified in 3GPP2 C.S0005–A v6.0. It is represented in units of 0.25 seconds and ranges from –1296000 to 1296000, both values inclusive (corresponding to a range of –90 to 90 degrees).
Support LE audio broadcast source feature
Abstract level value for overall signal quality
Retrieve an abstract level value for the overall signal quality.Value is between 0 and 4 inclusive
To obtain location, coordinates, altitude, and satellite information, please enable positioning permission and turn on precise location.
Can\'t get location info. Allow access to location first.

Base station longitude, which is a decimal number as specified in 3GPP2 C.S0005–A v6.0. It is represented in units of 0.25 seconds and ranges from –2592000 to 2592000, both values inclusive (corresponding to a range of –180 to 180 degrees).
The identity of the closed subscriber group that the cell belongs to. 27–bit CSG Identity. Value is between 0 and 0x7FFFFFF inclusive

Indicates whether the cell is restricted to only CSG members. A cell not broadcasting the CSG Indication but reporting CSG information is considered a Hybrid Cell.true if the cell is restricted to group members only.\n
Returns human–readable name of the closed subscriber group operating this cell (Node–B).
Query whether or not the device supports concurrent station (STA) connections for make–before–break wifi to wifi switching.
Switch to clock mode for the time input.
Switch to text input mode for the time input.
The latest wireless protocols supported by the router.
The router matches the latest protocol standards of current devices.
Mobile Country Code in string format.
The installation package has a problem or is incomplete.
Mobile Network Code in string format.
To obtain the service cell information, you need to turn on the Location – Exact Location permission
There is no SIM card inserted into your device.
If you have inserted the SIM card, some devices may grant blank permissions for security reasons. Please enter the Settings page to check whether the Phone permission is correctly turned on.
To obtain mobile signal information, you need to enable the Phone (Device Information) permission
Start date selection: %1$s – End date selection: %2$s
NR(New Radio 5G) Cell Identity.The 36–bit NR Cell Identity in range [0, 68719476735].
To obtain the information of nearby cells, you need to turn on the Location – Exact Location permission
The application market has not been installed, please try to update the webpage

Signal base station

A signal base station is a wireless communication facility used to provide mobile phone communication services. It usually consists of a set of tower–supported antennas that are responsible for sending and receiving wireless signals to mobile devices.
The mobile phone base station is an important part of the mobile communication network. It is responsible for transmitting mobile phone signals to the mobile operator\'s switch and communicating with other base stations to maintain the continuity of mobile phone calls.

Why sometimes the base station data is empty

There may be many reasons why the base station data is sometimes empty, including:

  • Network Problem:When the mobile phone network signal is unstable or there is no network, the mobile phone cannot communicate with the base station, resulting in empty base station data. Users can try to move to an area with better network coverage or restart the phone to reacquire base station data.
  • Operator issues: The operator\'s base station may be out of order or undergoing maintenance, causing the mobile phone to be unable to obtain base station data normally. In addition, there may also be blind spots in the operator\'s network coverage. In this case, the user can contact the operator for help or wait for some time and try to get the data again.
  • Mobile phone problems: The mobile phone itself may have hardware or software problems that prevent it from properly obtaining base station data.
    For example, the communication module of the mobile phone may be faulty, or the mobile phone system may have vulnerabilities. In this case, users can try to restart the phone or update the phone system software to solve the problem.
  • Permission issues: Some mobile applications may not have sufficient permissions to access the network and obtain base station data.
    This usually requires the user to manually enable relevant permissions in the phone settings. Users can check and modify relevant permission settings in the mobile phone settings.
  • Geographic location issues: In some remote areas or places with dense urban buildings, mobile phones may not be able to receive base station signals normally, resulting in empty base station data acquisition.
    In this case, the user can try to move to another area or wait for a while and try to obtain the data again.

To sum up, there are many reasons why the mobile phone obtains empty base station data, which may involve issues such as network, operator, mobile phone, permissions and geographical location.
Users can try different solutions to solve the problem according to the specific situation.
The collection time is too short and the data is not saved.

Generally speaking, noise levels can be divided into the following levels:

  • 0–20 decibels:Very quiet environment, barely audible.
  • 20–40 dB: Quiet environment, suitable for sleeping or concentrating.
  • 40–60 dB: Normal speech and traffic noise.
  • 60–80 decibels: Noisy environments may cause hearing damage.
  • Above 80 decibels: A very noisy environment can cause serious damage to hearing.

According to different standards, noise values can be divided into different levels. The corresponding range values and usage scenarios of each level are as follows:

  • International standards : According to international standards, noise is divided into three levels. Level I (30~59 decibels): It can be tolerated, but it is uncomfortable. When it reaches 40 decibels, it starts to disturb sleep. Level II (60~89 decibels): Increased interference with the autonomic nervous system, making it difficult to listen. 85 decibels is the general requirement for hearing protection. Grade III (90~120 decibels): Significant damage to the nervous system, causing irreversible damage to the hearing organ.
  • The overall level of environmental noise in urban areas: According to the overall level of environmental noise in urban areas, noise is divided into six levels. Level 0 standard during the day is ≤50.0 dB and ≤40.0 dB at night; Level 1 standard during the day is 50.1~55.0 dB and 40.1~45.0 dB at night; the value difference at each level is 5 dB, and so on.
  • Noise requirements in different places:
    • Sleeping environment: The ideal quiet environment noise level is 30~40 Around decibels.
    • Schools and libraries: Noise levels are required to be below 50 decibels to avoid disturbing learning, working and reading.
    • Offices and workplaces: Noise levels should be kept between 50~60 decibels during the day and between 40~50 decibels at night.
    • Traffic areas and road sides: The noise limit during the day and at night is generally between 60~70 decibels.
    • Industrial areas and manufacturing plants: These locations often require higher noise levels to meet production and manufacturing needs.
  • Different levels of classification :
    • Category 4a and 4b: This is based on traffic arteries Classification of areas within a certain distance on both sides that need to prevent traffic noise from having a serious impact on the surrounding environment. Category 4a includes areas on both sides of highways, first–class highways, second–class highways, urban expressways, urban trunk roads, urban secondary trunk roads, urban rail transit (ground sections), and inland waterways; Category 4b refers to areas on both sides of railway trunk lines.

It should be noted that the above classification and range values only provide a certain reference, and the specific standards and ranges may vary from country to country and region. . In order to protect people\'s health and provide a suitable living and working environment, it is recommended to take appropriate noise control measures in corresponding places.
No application is available to open
The device does not support the satellite positioning function
The device does not support sensors
Allow app to send you notifications?
New Radio Absolute Radio Frequency Channel Number.
Toggle digital localization, you need to restart the app
Thanks for your interest.\nWe will share more once you\'re back online.
You are back online! Let\'s pick up where we left off
You are back online! Continue learning about %s
We\'ll send you a notification with a link to the advertiser site.
Open ad when you\'re back online.
Whether this device supports the Wifi Passpoint terms and conditions feature.
Physical cell id.Integer value in range [0, 1007]. Value is between 0 and 1007 inclusive.
The Noise Meter requires the Microphone permission and is used to acquire sounds in the environment. Enable?
Please turn on the microphone permission in the permission settings
Failed to write to external storage
Allow the app to write to external storage to save files
To obtain base station information, please enable positioning services first.
This service is not supported on your device.
The device does not support manual refresh of base station information.
Technology–specific signal strength in Arbitrary Strength Units, calculated from the strength of the pilot signal or equivalent.
1xRTT level in (Android) ASU. CDMA does not have a standard definition for ASU; Android defines it as the smaller of RSSI and Ec/Io results (for 1xRTT).
Technology–specific signal strength in dBm, which is the signal strength of the pilot signal or equivalent.
Signal to noise ratio. Valid values are 0–8.
RSSI in ASU. Asu is calculated based on 3GPP RSSI.
RSRP in ASU. Asu is calculated based on 3GPP RSRP.
Channel quality index, ranging from 0 to 15 (inclusive).
Table index for channel quality indicator ,Value is between 1 and 6 inclusive.
Signal received power in dBm,Range: –140 dBm to –43 dBm.
Received Signal Strength Indication (RSSI) in dBm Value range [–113, –51] (inclusive).
Signal–to–noise ratio in dB,Range: –20 dB to 30 dB.
Timing advance value for LTE, as a value in range of 0..1282.
Asu is calculated based on 3GPP RSRP.

List of CSI channel quality indicators (CQI) for all subbands. If the CQI report is for the entire wideband, a single CQI index is provided. If the CQI report is for all subbands, one CQI index is provided for each subband, in ascending order of subband index.
CSI channel quality indicator (CQI) table index. There are multiple CQI tables. The definition of CQI in each table is different.
CSI signal received power,Range: –156 dBm to –31 dBm.
CSI signal received quality,Range: –20 dB to –3 dB.
CSI signal–to–noise and interference ratio,Range: –23 dB to 23 dB.
Signal received power,Range: –156 dBm to –31 dBm.
Signal received quality,Range: –43 dB to 20 dB.
Signal–to–noise and interference ratio,Range: –23 dB to 40 dB.
Timing advance value for a one way trip from cell to device for NR in microseconds.
Asu is calculated based on 3GPP RSRP,RSCP in ASU 0..96, 255.

Mobile phone signal strength

Mobile phone signal strength refers to the strength of the wireless signal received by the mobile phone. Signal strength is usually expressed by a signal strength indicator (RSSI), which is a negative value in decibels (dBm). The smaller the value, the weaker the signal. Standards for normal cell phone signal strength may vary in different countries and regions. Generally speaking, a mobile phone signal strength between –40dBm and –85dBm is considered normal. The stronger the signal, the better the call quality and network connection speed.

Factors affecting mobile phone signal strength

  • Base station distance: Mobile phone signals are sent through base stations. The farther away from the base station, the weaker the signal strength. .
  • Wireless network coverage issues: The different levels of network coverage of operators will also affect the strength of mobile phone signals. In some places, there may be blind areas or weak areas in network coverage, resulting in poor mobile phone signal strength.
  • Power issue: Power mainly includes the transmit power of the base station and the receive power of the mobile phone. The greater the base station\'s transmit power, the stronger the mobile phone signal; the stronger the mobile phone\'s reception capability, the stronger the signal.
  • Environmental factors: Environmental factors such as building obstructions, weather conditions (such as rain, fog, etc.), electromagnetic interference, etc. will also affect mobile phone signal strength.
  • Antenna azimuth and downtilt angle: Improper setting of the antenna azimuth and downtilt angle will also affect the reception and coverage of mobile phone signals and need to be considered at the same time.
  • Problems with the mobile phone itself: The antenna, radio frequency, signal processing and other hardware of the mobile phone will also affect the signal strength of the mobile phone.

Methods to improve mobile phone signal quality

  • Mobile location: Try to stay away from buildings, crowds and other objects that may interfere with the signal , choose an open place or a high place, such as a mountain top or a high–rise building, to get a better signal.
  • Toggle Airplane Mode on and off: Try turning Airplane Mode on and off, which sometimes solves the problem.
  • Restart your phone: Turning your phone off and on again may help improve the signal.
  • Check the operator\'s network coverage: If you are in an area with poor network coverage, try to contact the operator or move to another area.
  • Avoid using metal protective cases: Metal protective cases may affect signal reception. If your phone uses a metal protective case, you can try to remove it.
  • Use a signal booster: If you often spend time in an area with poor signal, consider using a signal booster.
  • Avoid using electronic devices to interfere: Avoid using electronic devices such as laptops, iPads, and microwave ovens, as they may interfere with mobile phone signals.
  • Adjust the position of your phone’s antenna: Avoid blocking the antenna when holding your phone to keep the signal open.
Asu is calculated based on 3GPP RSVP,RSVP in ASU 0..96, 255.
Ec/No (Energy per chip over the noise spectral density) as dB,measured cell in the range [–24, 1].

Single integer from 0 to 4 representing the general signal quality. This may take into account many different radio technology inputs. 0 represents very poor signal strength while 4 represents a very strong signal strength.

Ping is a network tool used to test the reliability and response time of network connections. It detects network connectivity between two computers by sending small data packets to the target host and waiting for its response.

Common uses include:

  • Verify connection to a remote computer: The ping command only works when the target host is powered on and the network connection is functioning properly. To succeed. In this way, it can be determined whether the target host exists.
  • Detecting network faults: When a network fault occurs, you can use the ping command to predict the fault and determine the location of the fault. If the ping command is unsuccessful, it may indicate a problem with the network cable, network card, or IP address.
  • Check the network connection speed: The ping command can measure the round–trip time and delay of data packets in the network, which helps to understand the connection speed and performance of the network.
  • Assess network stability: Continuous ping commands can be used to check whether the network is stable and whether there are disconnections or frequent delays.
  • Diagnosing other problems: Some specific ping command parameters and techniques can be used to diagnose other network problems, such as checking the configuration of a router or determining whether a specific network device is functioning properly.

The principle of the Ping command is that every machine on the network has a unique IP address. If you send a data packet to the target IP address, the other party will return a data packet of the same size. The data packets can determine the existence of the target host and even preliminarily determine the operating system of the target host.

Please note that although the ping command is a very useful tool, it cannot test all network connection problems. For example, it cannot test all paths between the local computer and the target host, nor can it test complex network configuration issues. Therefore, when troubleshooting network problems, you may need to combine other tools and methods for a comprehensive diagnosis.
Does this adapter support advanced power/performance counters
Whether the adapter supports offload connection scanning

Thank you for your trust and use. Please read carefully and fully understand the provisions of the User Agreement and Privacy Policy, including but not limited to: In order to provide you with better services, we need to collect information such as your device logo, operation logs, etc. Application performance. Please read 《%s》 and 《%s》 for details. \n If you agree with the content of the above agreement, please click 《Agree》 to start using the application.
There is no base station information. Please try to refresh the data.
1. 10 points can be redeemed for 1 hour of ad–free experience. The system will automatically redeem additional time when your current ad–free duration runs low.
2. Redeemed ad–free duration is calculated in natural hours. It takes effect immediately upon redemption and will count down normally even when the app is not open.
3. Points are only deducted when you use the app and trigger automatic redemption; no points will be consumed when the app is not in use.
4. The ad–free benefits redeemed with points only cover in–app banner, native, and interstitial ads, and do not include cold–start splash ads.
1. Watch a rewarded video ad to earn 10 points each time.
2. Points will be automatically credited once you watch a rewarded video ad and successfully claim the reward, and you can check your points on the page.
Watch rewarded videos to earn points and redeem an in–app ad–free experience
Signal received power is measured in dBm, ranging from –140 dBm to –43 dBm.
Received signal strength indicator. RSSI in dBm (–113, –51)
Received signal strength indication (RSSI) in dBm range [–113, –51]
Get reference signal–to–noise ratio (dB) range: –20dB to 30 dB.
Allow Ad to store image in Picture gallery?
Allow Ad to create a calendar event?
To obtain satellite data information, please enable positioning services first
To obtain satellite data information, please enable positioning permission and turn on precise location.
To obtain Bluetooth devices near the user, positioning permission needs to be enabled.
The acceleration sensor, the unit is m/s2, measures the acceleration applied to the X, Y, and Z axes of the device
A constant describing a limited axes accelerometer sensor.
Constants describing an uncalibrated finite–axis accelerometer sensor.
Constants describing an uncalibrated accelerometer sensor.

A constant describing an uncalibrated rotation vector sensor type.Identical to TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR except that it doesn\'t use the geomagnetic field. Therefore the Y axis doesn\'t point north, but instead to some other reference, that reference is allowed to drift by the same order of magnitude as the gyroscope drift around the Z axis.In the ideal case, a phone rotated and returning to the same real–world orientation should report the same game rotation vector (without using the earth\'s geomagnetic field). However, the orientation may drift somewhat over time.

A constant describing a geo–magnetic rotation vector.Similar to TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR, but using a magnetometer instead of using a gyroscope. This sensor uses lower power than the other rotation vectors, because it doesn\'t use the gyroscope. However, it is more noisy and will work best outdoors.
The geomagnetic rotation vector sensor provides the rotation vector of the mobile phone. When the mobile phone is in a sleep state, it can still record the orientation of the device
The gravity sensor, the unit is m/s2, measures the gravity applied to the X, Y, and Z axes of the device
Uncalibrated gyroscope sensor, providing raw, uncalibrated, compensated gyroscope data for post–processing and fusion positioning data
A constant describing a gyroscope sensor type.
A constant describing a limited axes gyroscope sensor.
A constant describing an uncalibrated limited axes gyroscope sensor.
Gyroscope sensor, the unit is rad/s, which measures the angular acceleration of the x, y, and z axes of the device
A constant describing a head tracker sensor.
A constant string describing a heading sensor.
A constant describing a heart beat sensor.
A constant describing a heart rate monitor.The reported value is the heart rate in beats per minute.
A constant describing a hinge angle sensor.
Humidity sensor, the unit is %, to measure the relative humidity of the surrounding environment
Light sensor, unit lx, detect the surrounding light intensity
Linear acceleration sensor, the unit is m/s2, this sensor is the data obtained by removing the influence of gravity from the acceleration sensor
A constant describing a low latency off–body detect sensor.
Magnetic sensor, the unit is uT (micro Tesla), measuring the magnetic field of three physical axes (x, y, z) around the device
Uncalibrated magnetic sensor, providing raw, uncalibrated magnetic field data
A constant describing a motion detect sensor.
Orientation sensor, which measures the rotation angle of the device around three physical axes (x, y, z)

A constant describing a pose sensor with 6 degrees of freedom. Similar to TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR, with additional delta translation from an arbitrary reference point. See SensorEvent.values for more details. Can use camera, depth sensor etc to compute output value. This is expected to be a high power sensor and expected only to be used when the screen is on. Expected to be more accurate than the rotation vector alone.
Pressure sensor, the unit is hPa (hectopascal), returns the pressure in the current environment
The distance sensor, in cm, is used to measure the distance from an object to the screen
A constant describing a relative humidity sensor type.
rotation vector sensor, the rotation vector represents the orientation of the device
A constant describing a stationary detect sensor.
The walking detection sensor triggers an event every time the user takes a step
1. Why are there multiple identical sensors in mobile phones?

The purpose of having multiple identical sensors in a phone is to perform different functions and provide more accurate data. Multiple identical sensors can provide redundancy and backup to ensure data reliability and stability. In addition, different models or configurations of mobile phones may have different sensor combinations to meet the needs of different users. Some sensors may be more focused on accuracy, while others may be more focused on responsiveness or stability. In addition, some sensors may be integrated in different components or modules to achieve more specific functions. For example, acceleration sensors can be used to detect the movement and posture of a device, or they can be used in conjunction with other sensors to achieve more complex functions such as gesture recognition or virtual reality interaction.
Game motion sensor, not affected by electromagnetic interference
Failed to share the installation package, please try "share link"
Retrieve an abstract level value for the overall signal strength.
SIM Card Error, present but faulty
SIM Card restricted, present but not usable due to carrier restrictions.
The carrier id of this subscription carrier.
The name displayed to the user that identifies subscription provider name. This name is the SPN displayed in status bar and many other places.
Data connection is active, but physical link is down.
Data connection activity: Currently receiving IP PPP traffic.
Data connection activity: Currently both sending and receiving IP PPP traffic.
Data connection activity: Currently sending IP PPP traffic.
Whether user enables data roaming for this subscription or not.
Data connection state: Connected. IP traffic should be available.
Data connection state: Currently setting up a data connection.
Data connection state: Disconnected. IP traffic not available.
Data connection state: Disconnecting. IP traffic may be available but will cease working imminently.
Data connection state: Handover in progress. The connection is being transited from cellular network to IWLAN, or from IWLAN to cellular network.
Data connection state: Suspended. The connection is up, but IP traffic is temporarily unavailable. For example, in a 2G network, data activity may be suspended when a voice call arrives.
Unique device ID used for subscription, such as IMEI for ESIM and MEID for CDMA phones.
The name displayed to the user that identifies this subscription. This name is used in Settings page and can be renamed by the user.
Used in scenarios where different subscriptions are bundled as a group. It\'s typically a primary and an opportunistic subscription.
Returns the ISO–3166–1 alpha–2 country code equivalent of the MCC (Mobile Country Code) of the current registered operator or the cell nearby, if available.
The radio technology (network type) that the device is currently using for data transmission.
An opportunistic subscription connects to a network that is limited in functionality and / or coverage.
The type of radio that transmits voice calls
MCC MNC (Mobile Country Code Mobile Network Code) of the SIM card provider. 5 or 6 decimal digits.
The type of a subscription. It can be Local–SIM Subscription or Remote–SIM Subscription.
This constant is to designate a subscription as a Local–SIM Subscription.\n \n A Local–SIM can be a physical SIM inserted into a sim–slot in the device, or eSIM on the device.
A Remote–SIM subscription is for a SIM on a phone connected to this device via some connectivity mechanism, for example bluetooth.
This subscription is forced to data–centric mode.

Subscription uses the default setting. The value is based upon device capability and the other properties of the subscription. Most subscriptions will default to voice–centric when in a phone. An opportunistic subscription will default to data–centric.

The usage setting is unknown. This will be the usage setting returned on devices that do not support querying the or setting the usage setting. It may also be provided by a carrier that wishes to provide a value to avoid making any settings changes.
This subscription is forced to voice–centric mode.
Your SIM card is PUK–locked. Type the PUK code to unlock it.
Unable to obtain speed information, please enable positioning services first.
To get speed information, please enable location permission and turn on precise location.
SS Reference signal received power.Range: –156 dBm to –31 dBm.
SS reference signal received quality.Range: –43 dB to 20 dB.
SS signal–to–noise and interference ratio, range: –23 dB to 40 dB.
Query whether or not the device supports concurrency of Station (STA) multiple access points (AP) (where the APs bridged together).
Whether the device supports local connection–only concurrent station (STA) connections

The magnetic field sensor of a mobile phone can indeed be used to measure environmental magnetic field values. However, due to the low sensitivity and resolution of the mobile phone magnetic sensor, there may be errors in the measured magnetic field values. . At the same time, the magnetic field of mobile phones will also be interfered by electronic equipment, metal objects and other surrounding environments, affecting the accuracy of magnetic field detection.
To obtain more accurate environmental electromagnetic values, it is recommended to use professional instruments and equipment for measurement.

International magnetic field standards are based on a Guidelines issued by the International Commission on Non–Ionizing Radiation Protection. The specified limits are: power frequency electric field 5000 volts per meter, power frequency magnetic field 100 microtesla.

Magnetic field strength is divided according to international standards and can be divided into the following levels. The range values and common scenarios of each level are as follows:

  • Very Low Field (VLF):Range values are typically 0.5–10 microTesla (µT). Commonly found in general environments, homes and industrial locations.
  • Low Field (LF): Range values are typically 10–100 microtesla (µT). Commonly found within the magnetic field generated by industrial equipment, household appliances, electronic equipment, etc.
  • Medium Magnetic Field (MF): Range values are typically 100–1000 microTesla (µT). Commonly found in the magnetic field generated by strong magnets, electromagnets, electric facilities, etc.
  • High Magnetic Field (HF): Range values are typically 1,000–10,000 microTesla (µT). Commonly used in special fields such as medical diagnosis and scientific research experiments.
  • Ultra High Magnetic Field (UHF): Range values greater than 10,000 microTesla (µT). Commonly used in special fields such as high–energy physics experiments and magnetic confinement nuclear fusion.

It should be noted that the specific division and range of magnetic field strength may vary depending on different applications and fields. At the same time, the impact of magnetic fields on the human body is also related to factors such as the direction and action time of the magnetic field. Therefore, when using magnetic field sensors, various factors need to be considered comprehensively and corresponding safety measures must be taken.
Pose sensor with 6 degrees of freedom

Traceroute is a route tracing utility. The traceroute command uses IP lifetime (TTL)Field and ICMP error message to determine the route from one host to other hosts on the network.

Traceroute is a network diagnostic tool used to determine the network path that a packet takes from a source host to a destination host. By analyzing the transmission of data packets between routers, the quality of the network connection can be judged, potential problems can be discovered, and targeted optimization can be carried out.

Common uses of traceroute include:

  • Analyzing network failures:When a website or server cannot be reached, traceroute can help determine The specific location on the network where the problem occurred. By tracking the path and timing of network packets, you can identify which router or network device may be causing the network failure and take appropriate steps to repair it.
  • Optimize network performance: By analyzing the results of route tracing, you can determine the number of routers that network data packets pass through during transmission, response time and other information, and perform network optimization. optimization. For example, you can choose to optimize network routing, shorten transmission paths, and reduce network delays, thereby improving network performance.
  • Secure your network: Traceroute can help identify the source of a network attack or hacker intrusion and harden your network. For example, some firewalls, intrusion detection and other measures can be taken to improve network security.

Please note that traceroute is a powerful tool, but it also requires certain skills and knowledge to use correctly. When using route tracing, it is recommended to seek professional help or refer to relevant technical documentation.

•Definition:\\nThe uplink frequency refers to the frequency used when the mobile phone sends data to the base station (i.e. \"uplink\"). In FDD mode, the uplink and downlink frequency intervals are fixed (called \"duplex interval\"); in TDD mode, the uplink and downlink share the frequency. \\n•Features:\\nDue to the limited transmission power of mobile phones, the selection of uplink frequency needs to consider signal propagation loss. \\nThe quality of the uplink signal affects the upload speed, voice call clarity, etc.

Wireless network protocol refers to the QoS protocol of WLAN, including 802.11a, 802.11b, 802.11e, etc.
This signal is not a real–time signal and its changes depend on the refresh rate of the WiFi list.
Calculates the level of the signal.

Wi–Fi signal strength

The normal value of WiFi signal strength also varies from country to country and region, but generally speaking, normal WiFi Signal strength should be between –40dBm and –85dBm. Signal strength within this range can ensure good network connection speed and stability.

Factors that affect WiFi signal strength

Mainly include the following points:

  • Distance:The distance from WiFi hotspots The further away, the weaker the signal strength. Therefore, the strength of the WiFi signal is affected by the distance from the router.
  • Obstacles: Obstacles such as walls, furniture, doors and windows may hinder the transmission of WiFi signals, causing the signal strength to weaken.
  • Weather conditions: Rain, snow, fog and other weather conditions may also affect the transmission of WiFi signals.
  • Router performance and location: Factors such as the router’s antenna gain, transmit power, placement, etc. will also affect the coverage and strength of the WiFi signal.
  • Interference from other devices: Electromagnetic waves from other wireless devices such as microwave ovens, wireless cameras, wireless mice, etc. may interfere with the transmission of WiFi signals.

If the WiFi signal strength is weak, it may cause problems such as slow, unstable or dropped network connections. In order to enhance the WiFi signal strength, you can try to move closer to the router or replace it with a high–performance router. Additionally, regularly restarting your router can help improve signal strength and network connection quality.
This is the protocol version currently connected to the device and the router. It may not be the latest protocol version supported by the router.

In order to provide better router services, we recommend the following operations for you to easily control your home router: First, enter the router management interface. The login IP address and account information are printed on the label on the back of the router; Second, after successful login, Click [DHCP Server]–[Client List] in sequence, and all devices connected to the router will be displayed. At this time, you can check the connected device information. If there are redundant devices, it means that the network is blocked. In addition, there is another way to use security software or the mobile APP that comes with the router to detect network interference on the current device. You can use the [wifi signal]–[WLAN squatting] tool to check the device connection status of the router WiFi.

If your daily access router equipment is relatively fixed, you can directly use MAC address binding to completely solve the problem of network rubbing. MAC address binding is to bind the MAC address of your commonly used Internet devices (such as laptop, mobile phone or tablet) with the router. The router only allows the bound devices to access, while the unbound devices can\'t access the Internet even if they break the wireless password or connect directly to the router. 1010So, how is the MAC address bound to the router?1010Step 1: enter the router setting interface and find the MAC address filtering or MAC address binding options in [advanced settings] – [network parameters] (the setting interfaces of routers of different brands are different, but you can find them in the advanced setting options). 1010Step 1: enter the router setting interface and find the MAC address filtering or MAC address binding options in [advanced settings] – [network parameters] (the setting interfaces of routers of different brands are different, but you can find them in the advanced setting options). Step 2: if there is the [enable MAC filtering function] option, enable this function, select the devices in the Allow list to connect to the network, and add the MAC address of the devices to be connected to the list. 1010The third step is to restart the router after setting.
SSID is the wireless network\'s name. Disabling SSID broadcast hides the SSID so others are unable to search for or access the network.

DHCP stands for Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol. Based on DHCP, your router dynamically assigns an IP address (such as 192.168.0.108) to a device connected to the network. You need to manually assign an IP address after disabling DHCP on your router. A rare IP address helps improve the overall security of your wireless network as it prevents others from cracking your router or using the network even if a connection is established.

In addition to the above methods, there are some auxiliary methods, such as reducing the transmission power of the router without affecting the Internet experience; Modify the default management address of the wireless router; Modify the management user name and password of the router; Set the WiFi password type of the router to wap–psk / wpa2–psk, and enter a password of 8 ~ 63 characters. You can add some unpopular characters or case.
Is this adapter supports Tunnel Directed Link Setup
